stdout:
## Acceptance Criteria

Pass. `gate-recency` is parsed as a graph assertion with `max-age-days`/`max-age-hours`, evaluated against `GateState.updated_at`, and receives an injected `now` in [crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:297). Missing and stale gates produce attributed findings naming the gate in [crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:883) and [crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:891). The fresh-evidence example blocks completion through `state = "done"`, `severity = "error"`, and `enforce = true` in [docs/examples/fresh-evidence/rules.toml](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/docs/examples/fresh-evidence/rules.toml:36).

## Architecture and Separation of Concerns

Pass. The pure graph engine stays in `validation/graph.rs`; wall-clock reads are injected from command boundaries in [crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs:403) and [crates/jit/src/commands/mod.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/mod.rs:549). I did not find storage or CLI formatting concerns leaking into the validation engine.

## Functional Paradigm

Pass. The evaluator uses iterator pipelines for rule and issue traversal in [crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:869), consistent with the local style.

## Correctness and Invariants

Pass. The prior overflow defect is fixed: `max-age-days` now uses `checked_mul(24)` and rejects values above `i64::MAX` before constructing `Assertion::GateRecency` in [crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs:994). That makes the evaluator’s `max_age_hours as i64` comparison safe for accepted configs.

## Safety

Pass. No unsafe code or PID handling changes were introduced in this issue scope.

## Testing

Pass. Provided gate evidence shows `cargo-ci` passed at commit `9e5f7b438f8fc264b8ff6dd2ac1839667c79251c` with `cargo fmt --all -- --check && cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test --workspace`.

Focused local checks also passed:

- `cargo test -p jit validation::rules::tests::test_gate_recency --quiet`
- `cargo test -p jit validation::graph::tests::test_gate_recency --quiet`
- `cargo test -p jit --test example_rulesets_tests fresh_evidence --quiet`
- `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed with allowed label warnings

The new regression test for huge parseable `max-age-days` is in [crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs:1968).

## Error Handling

Pass. Oversized recency configs now return contextual `RuleConfigError::InvalidAssertion` instead of silently saturating or wrapping, in [crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs:996).

## CLI Contract

Pass. No new command surface was added; existing validation and transition paths keep their JSON behavior.

## Documentation

Pass. The example ruleset documents the intended blocking behavior in [docs/examples/fresh-evidence/rules.toml](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/docs/examples/fresh-evidence/rules.toml:15). Public API documentation for `evaluate_graph` includes the injected clock contract in [crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:297).

## Prior Review Feedback

Pass. The most recent failed review’s blocking issue, large-age overflow and unsafe cast semantics, has been addressed and covered by test.

## JIT Issue Management

Pass. `issue.dependencies` is empty, so there are no prerequisite issues to verify. `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed; label warnings are allowed by the review instructions.

VERDICT: PASS
---
Gate result: PASSED
